classObjective: Graph linear equations by making a table of values and by finding and plotting the x- and y-intercepts.
workedExamples: 4-1, Example 1 — Graph by Making a Table of Values
4-1, Example 2 — Graph Using x- and y-Intercepts
warmUp: Find the x- and y-intercepts of 2x + 3y = 12
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: table construction for linear equations; intercept computation; vertical and horizontal line cases
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice graphing linear equations using tables and intercept methods
independentPractice: Practice problems graphing linear equations including special cases (horizontal and vertical lines)
assessment: Exit ticket: graph 4x - 2y = 8 using the intercept method
capReflection: Why might you choose the intercept method over making a table when graphing a linear equation?
